reference_guidelines.description: Model the sales staff as an Actor classifier.
reference_guidelines.citation: The sales staff are responsible for placing customer orders, with each order being assigned to a specific employee.

reference_guidelines.description: Model the customer as an Actor classifier.
reference_guidelines.citation: For each customer in an order, the system records their ID, name, phone number, delivery address, email, and the date of first contact with the company.

reference_guidelines.description: Model the marketing department employee as an Actor classifier.
reference_guidelines.citation: Only employees in the marketing department are authorized to update wine information and types.

reference_guidelines.description: Model the external system 'Gefen' as an Actor classifier.
reference_guidelines.citation: At the start of each month, the system will receive XML data from another system, called 'Gefen,' regarding wine producers and the wines they produce.

reference_guidelines.description: Model the external delivery company 'GalGalim' as an Actor classifier.
reference_guidelines.citation: The actual delivery of orders is managed by an external company, called 'GalGalim,' and falls outside the scope of the current system.

reference_guidelines.description: Model placing a regular order as a UseCase classifier.
reference_guidelines.citation: Regular orders, on the other hand, allow multiple customers to combine their wine selections into a single order to minimize delivery costs.

reference_guidelines.description: Model placing an urgent order as a UseCase classifier.
reference_guidelines.citation: Urgent orders are processed with priority and must be placed by a single customer to ensure prompt delivery.

reference_guidelines.description: Model updating wine information and types as a UseCase classifier.
reference_guidelines.citation: Only employees in the marketing department are authorized to update wine information and types.

reference_guidelines.description: Model inventory management as a UseCase classifier.
reference_guidelines.citation: The 'Cheers' system is being developed ... to manage key business operations, including inventory management.

reference_guidelines.description: Model customer management as a UseCase classifier.
reference_guidelines.citation: The 'Cheers' system is being developed ... to manage key business operations, including ... customer management.

reference_guidelines.description: Model order processing and delivery as a UseCase classifier.
reference_guidelines.citation: The 'Cheers' system is being developed ... to manage key business operations, including ... order processing and delivery.

reference_guidelines.description: An Association between the Actor 'SalesStaff' and the UseCase 'PlaceRegularOrder'.
reference_guidelines.citation: The sales staff are responsible for placing customer orders ... Regular orders ... allow multiple customers to combine their wine selections.

reference_guidelines.description: An Association between the Actor 'SalesStaff' and the UseCase 'PlaceUrgentOrder'.
reference_guidelines.citation: Urgent orders are processed with priority and must be placed by a single customer...

reference_guidelines.description: An Association between the Actor 'Customer' and the UseCase 'PlaceRegularOrder'.
reference_guidelines.citation: Regular orders ... allow multiple customers to combine their wine selections.

reference_guidelines.description: An Association between the Actor 'Customer' and the UseCase 'PlaceUrgentOrder'.
reference_guidelines.citation: Urgent orders ... must be placed by a single customer...

reference_guidelines.description: An Association between the Actor 'MarketingEmployee' and the UseCase 'UpdateWineInformation'.
reference_guidelines.citation: Only employees in the marketing department are authorized to update wine information and types.

reference_guidelines.description: A Generalization where 'Employee' is the parent Actor and 'SalesStaff' and 'MarketingEmployee' are child Actors.
reference_guidelines.citation: Company employees are allowed to order wines ... Only employees in the marketing department ...

reference_guidelines.description: A Generalization where 'PlaceOrder' is the parent UseCase and 'PlaceRegularOrder' and 'PlaceUrgentOrder' are specialized UseCases.
reference_guidelines.citation: Orders may be categorized as either regular or urgent.

reference_guidelines.description: A SystemBoundary enclosing all UseCases of the Cheers system to define their scope.
reference_guidelines.citation: The 'Cheers' system is being developed ... to manage key business operations...

unoperationalized_domain_parts: Attribute-level requirements for orders and customers (e.g., order number, dates, status, customer contact details).
Detailed business rules for delivery batching, bottle limits, clarification status, refunds, and suspend/resume order behavior.
Data model details for wine producers, wine attributes (sweetness level, pairing links, images), storage locations, and inventory counts, and report formatting (JSON export schedule).

questions_to_language_advisor.question: Should external systems such as 'Gefen' and 'GalGalim' be modeled as Actors in the Use Case Diagram, or is there a different stereotype for external interfaces?
Should the relationship between 'PlaceOrder', 'PlaceRegularOrder', and 'PlaceUrgentOrder' be modeled as a UseCase generalization (specialization) or using Include/Extend constructs?

agent1_capabilities: How does an Include relationship differ structurally from an Extend relationship in UML Use Case Diagrams?
What constraints exist on the endpoints of Association links in Use Case Diagrams?
Can a UseCase directly generalize an Actor in a Use Case Diagram?
